skill.name: launch-strategy
skill.description: Plan SaaS product launches, feature announcements, Product Hunt campaigns, beta rollouts, and ongoing launch momentum across owned, rented, and borrowed channels.
skill.summary: Build phased product launch plans with channel strategy, launch checklists, and post-launch momentum.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography and system reconnaissance patterns, but the referenced lines are ordinary marketing guidance in SKILL.md. I found no executable code, prompt injection attempt, data exfiltration path, or system access instruction.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The flagged locations contain launch strategy prose, not cryptographic functions, hashes, encryption settings, or executable code. This is a false positive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown guidance and the cited lines contain product launch text only. There is no algorithm name, cryptographic operation, or runtime behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The flagged line describes a public marketing case study. It does not instruct the assistant to run discovery commands or collect host information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is a marketing example about a YouTube review and sales impact. No command, filesystem path, process query, or host reconnaissance appears there.

content.value_statement: Product launches often lose momentum when teams treat them as one announcement. This skill helps build phased launch plans, channel strategies, and follow-up actions.
actual_capabilities: Structures launches into internal, alpha, beta, early access, and full launch phases.
Maps owned, rented, and borrowed channels to launch goals.
Provides Product Hunt preparation and launch-day guidance.
Suggests post-launch actions for onboarding, retention, and follow-up.
Helps prioritize announcements for major, medium, and minor updates.
limitations: Does not execute campaigns, publish content, or manage ad platforms.
Does not validate market demand without user-provided audience data.
Does not replace legal, compliance, or public relations review.
Does not include templates for every industry or customer segment.

use_cases.title: Plan a SaaS Launch
use_cases.description: Create a phased plan that moves from early testing to public availability with clear channel priorities.

use_cases.title: Announce a Major Feature
use_cases.description: Turn a product update into a coordinated campaign across email, in-app messages, social posts, and blog content.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Product Hunt Campaign
use_cases.description: Organize listing assets, supporter outreach, launch-day engagement, and post-launch follow-up.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Launch Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a launch for [product or feature]. My audience is [audience], my timeline is [timeline], and my current channels are [channels].

prompt_templates.title: Choose Launch Channels
prompt_templates.scenario: Selecting owned, rented, and borrowed channels
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a channel strategy for launching [product]. Compare owned, rented, and borrowed channels based on my audience, resources, and goals.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Product Hunt Launch
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing for a Product Hunt campaign
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Product Hunt launch plan for [product]. Include preparation, launch-day engagement, supporter outreach, and follow-up steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design Ongoing Launch Momentum
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a six-month announcement strategy for [product]. Prioritize major updates, medium updates, and minor updates across channels.

output_examples.input: Plan a beta launch for a collaboration tool with a small email list and active LinkedIn audience.
output: Recommended phase: beta launch with controlled access.
Primary owned channel: email list with segmented onboarding messages.
Primary rented channel: LinkedIn posts that drive signups to the waitlist.
Borrowed channel: founder interviews with productivity newsletters.
Next step: invite batches of users and collect structured feedback.

output_examples.input: Help me announce a new analytics dashboard to existing customers.
output: Treat this as a major feature announcement.
Use an in-app banner, customer email, blog post, and social proof from beta users.
Create a short demo and update website pages that mention reporting.
Follow up with engaged accounts and track adoption after the announcement.

best_practices: Start with owned channels so launch attention can become a direct audience relationship.
Match launch effort to update size instead of over-promoting every small change.
Plan post-launch education before launch day so new users understand the product quickly.
anti_patterns: Relying only on social platforms without capturing email signups or owned audience data.
Launching once and stopping before follow-up, onboarding, and feedback loops begin.
Using Product Hunt without preparing assets, supporters, and launch-day engagement.

faq.question: What types of launches does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports SaaS product launches, feature releases, beta programs, early access campaigns, Product Hunt launches, and ongoing product updates.

faq.question: Can it create a launch checklist?
faq.answer: Yes. It can organize pre-launch, launch-day, and post-launch tasks based on the product and available channels.

faq.question: Does it help with Product Hunt?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers listing preparation, supporter outreach, launch-day engagement, and post-launch conversion steps.

faq.question: Does it write launch copy?
faq.answer: It can guide messaging and channel planning, but dedicated copywriting may require a separate writing workflow.

faq.question: What information should I provide first?
faq.answer: Provide the product, audience, timeline, existing channels, audience size, and whether this is a new product or feature update.

faq.question: Is it suitable for non-SaaS launches?
faq.answer: It can help with general launch planning, but its examples and strongest guidance focus on SaaS and software products.
